goal: Produce a deterministic, read-only census of every builder/src module against ONE explicit live root, builder/src/server.ts. Per module record: serverReachable, orchestratorReachable (diagnostic only), usedValueImporters, unusedValueImporters, typeImporters, testImporters, plus a derived summary state. runtime_value_connected means serverReachable via used value imports. Classify each non-live module as CONNECT, COLLAPSE, ARCHIVE or KEEP_STAGED with a one-line rationale. No source module is changed, wired, moved, archived or deleted. This contract only produces the map.
scope_out: Do not modify, wire, import, move, archive or delete any builder/src module. Do not change server.ts, the opus* motor, providers, db or any runtime path. Output is one script plus one report. The census decides classifications; it does not apply them.
claims: The only runtime entry in the repo is builder/src/server.ts (npm start = tsx src/server.ts); there is no bin, Procfile, Dockerfile or second start, so server.ts is the single explicit live root.

opusTaskOrchestrator is NOT a separate live root: it is value-imported by sandboxRealWrite, opusBuildPipeline and probeDryRun, and server.ts imports probeDryRun and sandboxRealWrite, so the orchestrator is already transitively serverReachable. Treating it as a co-equal root would falsely mark orchestrator-only modules as live.
A value import counts only if the imported binding is actually used in the importing module body; type-only, test-only and unused/side-effect-only imports are not a live connection.
A module value-imported only by a non-serverReachable module is non_live_value_referenced; WIRE-SLICE-001's dispatchDryRunSlice will create exactly this case.
assumptions: The single explicit live root is builder/src/server.ts. No live root is defined by glob; additional roots are added only when a real second process entry exists.
Codex's local tree is synced to current origin/main via git pull --ff-only before locking (it was one commit behind at fcef539; main was 9c01487), and baseline_ref is set to that synced commit.
The usage check is a documented heuristic without a full type-checker; re-exports and dynamic dispatch are noted as residual blind spots.
dod: tools/orphan-scan.cjs walks builder/src from the single explicit root builder/src/server.ts and emits, per non-test module: serverReachable (bool, transitive from server.ts via USED value imports), orchestratorReachable (bool, transitive from opusTaskOrchestrator, diagnostic only), usedValueImporters[], unusedValueImporters[], typeImporters[], testImporters[].
Each module gets a derived summary state: runtime_value_connected (= serverReachable true) | non_live_value_referenced | type_only_referenced | test_only_referenced | unreferenced.
The live root is a named constant in the scanner, never an opus* glob; orchestratorReachable is reported but never used to decide runtime_value_connected.
Output is deterministic and exposes a reusable connectivity function so WIRE-GATE-001 can require it as a library.
docs/ORPHAN-CENSUS-v0.1.md lists every module that is not runtime_value_connected, each with exactly one classification (CONNECT | COLLAPSE | ARCHIVE | KEEP_STAGED) and a one-line rationale.
cardConditionedDispatch.ts, workerPacketWlpAdapter.ts and preRegisteredClaims.ts appear with their measured fields and state; if not runtime_value_connected they are classified CONNECT.

The census reports totals per summary state, per classification and per family (cockpit / runtime / memory / release / opus / other), plus a count of modules that are orchestratorReachable but NOT serverReachable.
node tools/orphan-scan.cjs, task-lock verify and diff-check pass.
stop_conditions: Any need to modify, wire, move, archive or delete a builder/src module.
Any need to touch server.ts, the opus* motor, providers, db, package files or workflows.
prior_task_findings: Verified against commit 9c01487 (origin/main): the only runtime entry is server.ts. opusTaskOrchestrator is imported by sandboxRealWrite/opusBuildPipeline/probeDryRun, and server.ts imports probeDryRun and sandboxRealWrite, so the orchestrator is already serverReachable, not a separate root. The ~198-file cockpit/runtime/memory/release lattice has 0 import edges to the opus* motor. runPreflight hard-stops on a dirty tree and loads the contract only if present.
